The '''PX-4 Mobile Command Base''' was a tracked, tactical command center manufactured by [[Nen-Carvon]].

==Characteristics==
Measuring 21.8 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in length,<ref name="ISB2" /> the PX-4 Mobile Command Base was heavily armored and armed. Covered in a half meter thick metallic shell, the PX-4 had a command pod that was located in the center of the vehicle, encased in a further armored shell for maximum protection. The outer hull of the vehicle was covered in reflective shielding. [[Sensor/Legends|Sensors]] and [[Computer/Legends|computers]] monitored all aspects of a battle as it was fought, relaying the information to the commander in the command pod, and providing up-to-the-minute analysis and tactical suggestions.<ref name="ISB" /> Information was displayed via computer displays, [[Hologram/Legends|holographic]] tactical displays, and communications arrays.<ref name="FUCG" /> Communications was also monitored, from standard [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] channels, to enemy transmissions.<ref name="ISB" />

A single [[Mk 1e/S heavy laser cannon]] was mounted on the top of the PX-4,<ref name="ISB" /> on a slow-moving rotating turret.<ref name="ISB2" /> The weapon gave the PX-4 an offensive punch, but the vehicle was not intended to enter combat.<ref name="ISB" /> The PX-4 employed tracked treads to move about the battlefield;<ref name="ISB" /> speeds of 100 [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] an [[Standard hour/Legends|hour]] were common,<ref name="ISB2" /> with some vehicles being reported at being able to achieve 200 kilometers an hour.<ref name="FUCG" /> The PX-4 could carry a [[Metric ton/Legends|metric ton]] of cargo,<ref name="ISB2" /> and consumable supplies for a [[Standard week/Legends|week]].<ref name="FUCG" /> Requiring a crew of three, one of whom served as the [[Gunner/Legends|gunner]], the PX-4 had room enough for seven passengers.<ref name="ISB2" />

==History==
[[File:Mcbyavin.jpg|thumb|left|250px|A PX-4 escorted by stormtroopers]]
The PX-4 Mobile Command Base was employed by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] to provide safe transportation for [[Commander/Legends|commanders]] into hostile combat zones. It acted as a mobile headquarters, allowing the unit commander to travel with [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] without exposing themselves to potential danger.<ref name="ISB" /> Typically, the PX-4 was removed from the direct battlefield, allowing [[Officer/Legends|officers]] to make tactical and strategic decisions in safety. Deployment of the PX-4 was achieved through the use of [[Landing craft/Legends|landing craft]]. Commonly, [[Low Altitude Assault Transport/carrier/Legends|LAAT/c dropships]] were employed, although they could also be slung beneath [[Sentinel-class landing craft/Legends|''Sentinel''-class landing craft]].<ref name="FUCG" /> They were also part of the carried vehicle complement of the [[IF-120 landing craft]].<ref name="GaW">''[[Galaxy at War]]''</ref>

On some worlds that were openly hostile to the Empire, planetary [[Prefect/Legends|prefects]] and [[Governor/Legends|governors]] relied on the PX-4 to move around safely. Some were known to even live inside the PX-4, never leaving the armored interior.<ref name="ISB" />

[[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Grigor (governor)|Grigor]] employed a PX-4 during the [[Battle of Red Rock]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[M'haeli/Legends|M'haeli]]. The vehicle was destroyed by [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] forces during the fighting.<ref name="RoC4">[[River of Chaos 4|''River of Chaos'' 4]]</ref>

==Appearances==
*[[River of Chaos 4|''River of Chaos'' 4]]
